formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Since the chapter 'Rights' in Class 11 Political Science doesn't involve mathematical formulas, I'll provide key statements and laws relevant to the topic. Here's a JSON array with the necessary information:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Right to Equality",
    "formula": "Article 14 of the Indian Constitution states that the State shall not deny to any person equality before the law or the equal protection of the laws within the territory of India.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This right is enshrined in the Constitution to ensure that all citizens are treated equally under the law."
  },
  {
    "name": "Right to Freedom",
    "formula": "Article 19 of the Indian Constitution guarantees six freedoms to all citizens, including freedom of speech and expression, assembly, association, movement, residence, and occupation.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "These freedoms are subject to reasonable restrictions as specified in the Constitution."
  },
  {
    "name": "Right to Life and Liberty",
    "formula": "Article 21 of the Indian Constitution states that no person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This right is fundamental to the dignity and well-being of every individual."
  },
  {
    "name": "Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression",
    "formula": "Article 19(1)(a) of the Indian Constitution guarantees the freedom of speech and expression, but this right is subject to reasonable restrictions as specified in Article 19(2).",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "The right to freedom of speech and expression is essential for the functioning of a democratic society."
  },
  {
    "name": "Right to Education",
    "formula": "Article 21-A of the Indian Constitution makes education a fundamental right of every child between the ages of 6 and 14.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This right is aimed at ensuring that every child has access to quality education."
  }
]
```

Note: The formulas provided are statements of laws and key concepts related to the topic of 'Rights' in Class 11 Political Science.
